pattern: Comparing candidate integers against a value avoids floating point by scaling the average through the element count instead of dividing.
hints: You are looking for the smallest positive integer that both exceeds the average and is not present in the array.
Start counting from the first integer strictly above the average and skip any value already in the array.
Put the elements in a set; let m = floor(average) + 1 be the first candidate, then increment past any candidate that is in the set.

edge_inputs.description: average is an integer and the next integer is present
candidate just above average is taken so must skip
all negatives make the average negative
consecutive run forces a skip chain
single element equal to its own average

stress.description: random small arrays in the full value range to vary the average and presence gaps
stress.note: small range guarantees the answer is found within a few candidates
